positional_bias.note: Derived positions from .gift match sidecar correct_position_distribution exactly.

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors score 2–4 on the 1–4 plausibility scale; no score-1 (obviously absurd) distractors found. Two distractors in q2 (pros/cons before job, deliberate word choice) scored 2 (wrong but unrelated to core S1 misconceptions) — below absurd threshold.

learning_objectives: Explain what System 1 thinking is and describe its three defining characteristics: speed, automaticity, and low effort.
Give at least two everyday examples of System 1 thinking in action.
Distinguish System 1 from System 2 thinking at a high level (detailed coverage of System 2 is in topic 9.2).
Explain what a cognitive heuristic is and why heuristics are useful.
Describe why System 1's speed and automation can sometimes produce errors.
Identify one way System 1 thinking is relevant when a person evaluates AI-generated output.

verdict: Pass — all four bias / coverage checks clean.
